title: Duros Sector

The '''Duros Sector''' was a district of [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]]. It was inhabited throughout its existence primarily by [[Duros/Legends|Duros]], hence its name. It was one of the [[Moon/Legends|moon's]] poorest regions.

==History==
===Old Republic Era===
During the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]], the sector was also inhabited by a variety of alien war refugees from across the galaxy seeking shelter from the expanding [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]]. Because of this, it was the location of much resistance to Imperial rule.

[[Madel Felth]], an [[Imperial Intelligence (Sith Empire)|Imperial Intelligence]] officer, was charged with leading operations against the rebels in the sector. The rebellion had the direct support of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], which sent several Knights to join the fight against the Empire.

===Rise of the Empire Era===
In the decades prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], it received large-scale funding from the [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidians]] of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] and was known throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] as a cosmopolitan haven of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. It contained the [[Old Duros Spaceport]] and a secret tunnel entrance into the [[Corellian Sector (Nar Shaddaa)/Legends|Corellian Sector]].

After the fall of the Trade Federation and the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the Duros Sector fell into disrepair and malaise, assuming the character of the rest of the [[Moon/Legends|moon]]. Many Neimoidians living there assumed the identity of [[Duros/Legends|Duros]]. By the early years of the Empire, the sector had become abandoned, inhabited only by semi-intelligent predators. It then became known as the '''Old Duros Sector'''.
